verses.text: 'The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?' Though an army encamp against him, David remains confident. God Himself banishes his dread. When the Lord is our light and stronghold, no foe can finally daunt us.

'The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?' Though an army encamp against him, David remains confident. God Himself banishes his dread. When the Lord is our light and stronghold, no foe can finally daunt us.

'The LORD is my light and my salvation; whom shall I fear?' Though an army encamp against him, David remains confident. God Himself banishes his dread. When the Lord is our light and stronghold, no foe can finally daunt us.

His one desire is to dwell in God's house all his days and behold His beauty, for there God will hide him in trouble. Communion with God is his treasure. Above safety and success, the believer's deepest longing is for God Himself.

His one desire is to dwell in God's house all his days and behold His beauty, for there God will hide him in trouble. Communion with God is his treasure. Above safety and success, the believer's deepest longing is for God Himself.

His one desire is to dwell in God's house all his days and behold His beauty, for there God will hide him in trouble. Communion with God is his treasure. Above safety and success, the believer's deepest longing is for God Himself.

When God says, 'Seek ye my face,' his heart answers, 'Thy face, LORD, will I seek,' pleading that God not hide it. He responds to the invitation. Prayer is the soul echoing back God's own call to come near.

When God says, 'Seek ye my face,' his heart answers, 'Thy face, LORD, will I seek,' pleading that God not hide it. He responds to the invitation. Prayer is the soul echoing back God's own call to come near.

When God says, 'Seek ye my face,' his heart answers, 'Thy face, LORD, will I seek,' pleading that God not hide it. He responds to the invitation. Prayer is the soul echoing back God's own call to come near.
'When my father and my mother forsake me, then the LORD will take me up.' No earthly love is so sure. When the nearest fail us, God draws nearest of all.
He asks to be taught God's way and led in a plain path, kept from the will of his enemies. He seeks guidance and protection together. The believer wants both a right road and a safe one.
He asks to be taught God's way and led in a plain path, kept from the will of his enemies. He seeks guidance and protection together. The believer wants both a right road and a safe one.

He would have fainted but for believing he would 'see the goodness of the LORD in the land of the living,' and so he says, 'Wait on the LORD... and he shall strengthen thine heart.' Faith holds on for coming good. Waiting on God is not idleness but strength renewed.
